attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who capitalizes when GC teams relax control after major efforts. Demonstrates ability to recover and bridge back to leaders after being dropped, then wins from reduced groups.
team_tactics: Frequently deployed as high-altitude domestique for GC leaders, often left as sole support on critical climbs. When racing for himself, targets breakaways after 40km+ when peloton control loosens.
terrain_preference: Excels on hybrid terrain requiring both climbing and classics skills. Superior climbing recovery allows him to survive medium-mountain stages then compete in reduced sprints. Vulnerable in pure sprint finishes.
vulnerability: Poor sprint management—empties completely in final 100m when going too early. Can be dropped on initial climb accelerations, requiring energy-costly efforts to bridge back. Descending has shown reckless decision-making.
key_patterns: Gets dropped initially on climbs but bridges back through superior recovery (2 instances)
Functions as last domestique for GC leaders on mountain stages (2 observations)
Wins from breakaways when GC team control relaxes after sustained effort
Poor final-100m management, attacking too early and emptying completely (2 instances)
Takes unnecessary risks on descents without tactical gain
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 showed classic strengths (recovery, breakaway win) but fundamental weaknesses (sprint management, reckless descending) persist across career
